expectations.evidence: Section 'CRITICAL-2 — Password Included in JWT Payload' — correctly identifies password in jwt.sign() payload as Critical
Section 'CRITICAL-3 — Wildcard CORS with Credentials Allowed' — correctly flags Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * + Allow-Credentials: true combo
Section 'CRITICAL-1 — Hardcoded Credentials' — flags admin/admin123 hardcoded comparison
MEDIUM-3 discusses 'JWT Claims Not Inspected in Middleware' (decoded payload not examined) but does NOT mention the missing { algorithms: ['HS256'] } option in jwt.verify() — different finding

LOW-1 is 'HTTPS Not Enforced' — does not mention missing CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, or any security headers by name. Criteria requires one of those specific headers to be mentioned.
Covers auth route (backend) and CORS config in next.config.js (config/frontend-adjacent). Also mentions HTTPS and CSRF. Satisfies 'both API route AND configuration-level issues' criteria.
